strategy_canvas.headline_thesis: Elitez LMS owns the BYO-deck reverse-engineering wedge at S$99–S$299 platform-flat — a band where Cornerstone/Workday/SAP won't quote, Coursebox/Articulate don't reverse-engineer, and HRMS-bundled Learn modules don't author.

dimensions.label: AI quiz gen from your content

segments.name: SME Ops Director with decks
segments.descriptor: 30–120 HC SG SME with existing decks, no LMS, low procurement friction

segments.name: EA-agency Operations Head
segments.descriptor: SG EA-licensed agency, compliance + role-onboarding heavy

segments.name: HRMS / EOR partner CS Lead
segments.descriptor: SG HRMS or EOR provider wanting white-label learning bundle

segments.name: Mid-market HR Manager (100–500 HC)
segments.descriptor: Branded SG mid-market firm with role-tracks + heatmap appetite

segments.name: Brand-led SME owner
segments.descriptor: 40–250 HC SG firm where brand IP (brand book, product catalogue) is core

competitors.name: Coursebox AI
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Doc-to-course works; but outputs generic — SME deck voice doesn't survive.

competitors.name: Articulate 360
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Expects slide-by-slide manual rebuild; no SME self-serve ingest path.

competitors.name: iSpring Learn
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: PPT-import is 1:1 only, not modular reverse-engineering.

competitors.name: DIY SharePoint+Forms
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Decks already there — but no structure, no quiz, no progress.

competitors.name: Coursebox AI
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: AI quiz gen present; quality variable on SME jargon-heavy decks.

competitors.name: Articulate 360
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: AI Assistant rewrites text; doesn't auto-generate quiz against source doc.

competitors.name: Talenox Learn
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Quizzes are checklist-shaped; no AI generation against source content.

competitors.name: DIY SharePoint+Forms
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Forms quiz is fully hand-built; no AI seed from the SOP deck.

competitors.name: Coursebox AI
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Self-serve Pro tier (US$99/mo); accessible to SME owner directly.

competitors.name: Articulate 360
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: US$1,399/yr author license trips finance procurement at most SG SMEs.

competitors.name: TalentLMS
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Free tier + S$89/mo Basic — SME owner can swipe a card.

competitors.name: DIY SharePoint+Forms
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Already on the M365 bill — zero new procurement decision.

competitors.name: Talenox Learn
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Some SG compliance content; thinner on EA-Act + TAFEP than the Elitez library.

competitors.name: iSpring Learn
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: No SG baseline at all; channel partner sometimes ships compliance packs.

competitors.name: Skillgrasp
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: WSQ-aligned SG packs; not EA-Act-recruiter-specific.

competitors.name: DIY SharePoint+Forms
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Buyer assembles compliance content from MOM/PDPC PDFs themselves.

competitors.name: Coursebox AI
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Can ingest agency interview-prep + sourcing decks; voice drift typical.

competitors.name: iSpring Learn
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Imports recruiter sales decks 1:1 but doesn't structure as role-modules.

competitors.name: Talenox Learn
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: No ingest; agency must copy-paste into checklist authoring tool.

competitors.name: Articulate 360
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Powerful authoring but agency Ops Head can't be the author at scale.

competitors.name: Coursebox AI
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Generates quiz but no EA-Act-specific guardrails or role-track scoping.

competitors.name: Disprz
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Author AI can ingest SCORM/docs; role-targeting requires Disprz skills model setup.

competitors.name: iSpring Learn
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Quizzes hand-authored only; AI assist is text-rewrite, not gen.

competitors.name: Skillgrasp
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Hand-authored quizzes; SG packs ship pre-built only.

competitors.name: Docebo
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Strong white-label but enterprise procurement scale; bundle economics break.

competitors.name: TalentLMS
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Multi-tenant for partners; per-tenant pricing inflates for HRMS bundling.

competitors.name: Disprz
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: White-label available; minimum-seat floors hostile to SG-SME partner customers.

competitors.name: Talenox Learn
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Partner side of equation — building their own white-label, not consuming.

competitors.name: Coursera (SG catalog)
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Coursera catalog has SG-track content but uneditable + not partner-brandable.

competitors.name: Skillgrasp
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: SG packs exist; smaller catalog than Elitez 56-module baseline.

competitors.name: KnowBe4
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Strong on security only; HR compliance not in scope.

competitors.name: Talenox Learn
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Some compliance content native; not exposed to partner-distribution.

competitors.name: Docebo
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Strong analytics including skill-graph drill-down; price out of reach for 100–500 HC.

competitors.name: Cornerstone Learn
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Enterprise analytics best-in-class; gated behind 500-seat minimum procurement.

competitors.name: TalentLMS
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Quiz analytics decent; per-question retry heatmap less developed.

competitors.name: DIY SharePoint+Forms
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Forms shows % correct per quiz; no per-question failure heatmap surface.

competitors.name: Articulate 360
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Brand-precise authoring; pair with separate LMS for delivery — double-cost.

competitors.name: TalentLMS
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Custom theme available; full white-label gated to higher tier + per-tenant cost.

competitors.name: Docebo
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Excellent white-label; pricing structure shuts out mid-market wallet.

competitors.name: Talenox Learn
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Logo only; learner-facing remains Talenox-branded.

competitors.name: Articulate 360
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Authoring fidelity high; delivery needs separate LMS — double brand-control burden.

competitors.name: LearnDash (WP)
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Full WP design freedom; tech maintenance burden falls on brand-precious SME.

competitors.name: Docebo
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: White-label clean; price-shape impossible for 40–250 HC brand-led SME.

competitors.name: DIY SharePoint+Forms
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Microsoft-branded learner surface — kills the brand-immersion ambition.

competitors.name: Coursebox AI
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Doc-to-course ingest works; AI rewrites brand voice into generic AI prose.

competitors.name: iSpring Learn
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: 1:1 PPT import preserves brand visuals but no modular restructuring.

competitors.name: Articulate 360
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Authoring is rebuild-from-scratch; brand-led SME has no L&D author capacity.

competitors.name: Synthesia
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Slide-to-video is real but output is video; no quiz/module shell to live in.

attack_plans.icp: SG SME (30–120 HC) Ops Director who already has 5–8 decks (onboarding, SOP, brand book, sales pitch) sitting in SharePoint / Google Drive.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: ~33% of the S$30M mid-market TAM stack — SG services firms with deck-led training (DOS Services Sector Survey 2024 + industry triangulation).
attack_plans.why_gap: Articulate/iSpring expect manual rebuild; Coursebox/Sana generate generic AI text; HRMS Learn modules don't author at all; DIY SharePoint has no AI ingest pathway. Nobody at sub-S$300/mo platform-flat does deck-in → modular-out.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Pro tier ($99 + $2.99/seat) sits in SME impulse-buy wallet; reverse-engineering returns first usable module within hours; SG library covers the white-space the SME's own decks don't (compliance, AI literacy).

gtm.channel: Direct outbound + LinkedIn content + EA-agency network word-of-mouth
gtm.pitch: Drop your decks; we'll turn them into modules you can publish this week.
gtm.pricing: Pro tier; S$99 platform + S$2.99/seat (typical 30–80 seats = S$190–S$340/mo)
gtm.content: 60-second screencast of deck → module transformation; case study from first 5 paying customers; SG-context examples in every prospect demo.

attack_plans.icp: SG EA-licensed recruitment agency (15–150 staff) Operations Head with role-track onboarding pain across Recruitment Consultant, BD Executive, Account Manager, HR Ops roles.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: ~1,200 SG EA-agencies × ~S$3K/yr typical wallet × estimated 80% addressable = ~S$2.9M, conservatively rounded.
attack_plans.why_gap: EA-Act + TAFEP + Fair Consideration + CPF + work-passes is mandatory but no global LMS ships SG-tuned recruiter-craft content. Skillgrasp is closest but smaller catalog. Talenox Learn is checklist-shaped not authoring.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Elitez heritage IS staffing — onboarding-plan.json role-tracks (Recruitment Consultant / BD Executive / Account Manager / HR Ops) ship out-of-box; 80% of agency need solved on day one; remaining 20% loaded via BYO upload.

gtm.channel: MOM EA-agency directory direct outbound + IHRP / SARA partnerships + EA-licence renewal cycle timing
gtm.pitch: Your new recruiter is productive in 4 weeks not 12 — your compliance evidence is audit-ready in one click.
gtm.pricing: Team or Pro tier (S$2.99/seat + optional S$99 platform); typical 30-seat agency = S$190/mo
gtm.content: EA-Act compliance checklist PDF (lead magnet); recruiter-onboarding playbook; testimonials from Elitez group + 3 EA-agency case studies.

attack_plans.icp: SG HRMS provider (Talenox, JustLogin) or EOR provider (Multiplier SG-HQ, Deel SG, Velocity Global) wanting a credible learning module to attach to their stack without 12-month in-house build.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: ~40% of S$14M provider-tenancy SAM stack — HRMS + EOR partners with realistic 200-end-customer attach rate over 24 months.
attack_plans.why_gap: Articulate / Cornerstone / Docebo white-label exists but enterprise pricing destroys bundle economics. Coursebox not partner-shaped. HRMS Learn modules built in-house are checklist-shaped not authoring.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Enterprise tier ($299 platform + $2.99/seat) means partner can re-price the end-customer with material margin; white-label + custom domain + SSO + API are first-class; SG hosting clears partner PDPA gates.

gtm.channel: Direct relationships with Talenox / JustLogin / Multiplier (SG-HQ) / Deel SG CS leads; channel-enablement collateral
gtm.pitch: Your Learn module, our engine — ship in 4 weeks, white-label, your brand, your domain.
gtm.pricing: Enterprise tier with partner-margin discount on flat fee; per-seat fixed at S$2.99
gtm.content: Channel-partner one-pager; integration spec; 24-month pilot economics model showing partner attach economics.

attack_plans.icp: SG mid-market firm (100–500 HC) HR Manager with budget (S$10–25K/yr) but no patience for enterprise implementation cycles.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: Sub-enterprise SAM stack — SG firms 500–2,000 HC not locked into Workday/SAP/Cornerstone × 60% addressable.
attack_plans.why_gap: TalentLMS/iSpring/360Learning compete on per-seat but lack reverse-engineering + AI quiz from your content. Cornerstone/Workday/SAP gate below 500 seats. Heatmap analytics are emerging table-stakes nobody ships well in the sub-S$300 platform band.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Scale tier ($199 platform + $2.99/seat) at 250 seats = S$947/mo — competitive with TalentLMS Plus but with reverse-engineering, AI quiz gen, role-tracks, knowledge-gap heatmaps the others lack at this band.

gtm.channel: LinkedIn outbound + IHRP HR Manager community + analyst (Gartner Cool Vendors APAC) + case-study-driven content
gtm.pitch: Know which questions your staff fail before the audit asks — and re-skin the platform with your brand in a day.
gtm.pricing: Scale tier; S$199 platform + S$2.99/seat; quarterly business review included
gtm.content: Knowledge-gap-heatmap product video; mid-market HR Manager playbook; analyst-shopped benchmarks vs TalentLMS / iSpring / 360Learning.

attack_plans.icp: SG SME owner (40–250 HC) in professional services / retail / F&B / consumer brands where brand IP is core — training MUST feel native to the brand.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: Subset of mid-market + brand-led SME SAM — ~10% of S$27M SAM filtered to brand-precious buyers willing to pay for white-label.
attack_plans.why_gap: Articulate / iSpring need separate LMS (double-burden); Docebo / Cornerstone white-label gated behind enterprise. LearnDash gives WP design freedom but tech maintenance burden. DIY shows Microsoft branding.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Scale → Enterprise jump (S$199 → S$299 platform) is small relative to design agency line-item already paid; white-label + custom domain + custom theme = full brand control without WP-maintenance burden.

gtm.channel: Brand-led SME network (Singapore Brand Network, design-agency referrals) + creative-director-targeted LinkedIn
gtm.pitch: Your team's training looks like a chapter of your brand book — not a SaaS tool.
gtm.pricing: Enterprise tier (S$299 platform + S$2.99/seat) with optional one-time design-onboarding service
gtm.content: Brand-immersion case study (Elitez Group Of Companies own usage as proof); template gallery; designer-led webinar series.
